title: Elder Usabha: Trees grow tall when irrigated by high rain clouds, With new rain they become green and strong. For Usabha, delighting in seclusion, aware…
content:
Elder Usabha:

Trees grow tall when irrigated by high rain clouds,
With new rain they become green and strong.
For Usabha, delighting in seclusion, aware of the forest,
That one produces many good things.

title: Kandaraka, in this Saṅgha of bhikkhus, there are bhikkhus who are learners, steadfast in virtue, steadfast in good conduct, intelligent, and who live with their…
content: Kandaraka, in this Saṅgha of bhikkhus, there are bhikkhus who are learners, steadfast in virtue, steadfast in good conduct, intelligent, and who live with their minds well established in the Four Foundations of Mindfulness. What are the four? Here, Kandaraka, a bhikkhu dwells contemplating the body as a body, ardent, clearly comprehending, mindful, having removed covetousness and displeasure in regard to the world; he dwells contemplating feelings as feelings ... mind as mind ... phenomena as phenomena, ardent, clearly comprehending, mindful, having removed covetousness and displeasure in regard to the world.

title: Young householder, wandering the streets at improper times has six dangers: oneself is unprotected and unguarded, one's wife and children are unprotected and unguarded, one's…
content: Young householder, wandering the streets at improper times has six dangers: oneself is unprotected and unguarded, one's wife and children are unprotected and unguarded, one's property is unprotected and unguarded, one is suspected of evil deeds, one is subject to false rumors, and one encounters many troubles. Young householder, such are the six dangers of wandering the streets at improper times.

title: Nāgita, for those who are heavy with craving for pleasure, Nāgita, with the change and alteration of that, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair arise.…
content: Nāgita, for those who are heavy with craving for pleasure, Nāgita, with the change and alteration of that, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair arise. That is the natural consequence for that person. For those who focus their minds on the sign of the foul, Nāgita, disenchantment with the sign of the beautiful becomes established. That is the natural consequence for that person. For one who dwells contemplating impermanence in the six sense-spheres, Nāgita, disenchantment with contact becomes established. That is the natural consequence for that person. For one who dwells contemplating arising and passing away in the five aggregates of clinging, Nāgita, disenchantment with clinging becomes established. That is the natural consequence for that person.

title: Elder Mahā Moggallāna: We are forest dwellers, Living on almsfood, Content with the food Discarded in the bowl. We crush Māra and his army, If…
content:
Elder Mahā Moggallāna:

We are forest dwellers,
Living on almsfood,
Content with the food
Discarded in the bowl.
We crush Māra and his army,
If the mind is well concentrated.

We are forest dwellers,
Living on almsfood,
Content with the food
Discarded in the bowl.
We drive away Māra and his army,
Like an elephant [crushes] a hut of reeds.

We dwell at the foot of a tree,
Patient, with persistent mind,
Content with the food
Discarded in the bowl.
We crush Māra and his army,
If the mind is well concentrated.

We dwell at the foot of a tree,
Patient, with persistent mind,
Content with the food
Discarded in the bowl.
We drive away Māra and his army,
Like an elephant [crushes] a hut of reeds.

title: Bhikkhus, any samaṇas or brahmins who are not bound, not infatuated, not obsessed with these five strands of sensual pleasure, see the danger in them,…
content: Bhikkhus, any samaṇas or brahmins who are not bound, not infatuated, not obsessed with these five strands of sensual pleasure, see the danger in them, know the escape from them, and enjoy them — these should be understood as: 'They have not fallen into misfortune, not into calamity, and are not being used by Māra as he wishes.' Bhikkhus, just as a forest deer that has not fallen into a trap lies down — that deer should be understood as: 'It has not fallen into misfortune, not into calamity, and is not being used by the hunter as he wishes. When the hunter comes, the deer can leave as it wishes.' So too, bhikkhus, those samaṇas or brahmins who are not bound ... are not being used by Māra as he wishes.

title: And, bhikkhus, what is right view? Bhikkhus, knowledge of suffering, knowledge of the origin of suffering, knowledge of the cessation of suffering, knowledge of the…
content: And, bhikkhus, what is right view? Bhikkhus, knowledge of suffering, knowledge of the origin of suffering, knowledge of the cessation of suffering, knowledge of the path leading to the cessation of suffering. Bhikkhus, this is called right view. And, bhikkhus, what is right intention? Bhikkhus, intention of renunciation, intention of non-ill-will, intention of harmlessness. Bhikkhus, this is called right intention. And, bhikkhus, what is right speech? Bhikkhus, abstaining from false speech, abstaining from divisive speech, abstaining from harsh speech, abstaining from idle chatter. Bhikkhus, this is called right speech. And, bhikkhus, what is right action? Bhikkhus, abstaining from killing living beings, abstaining from taking what is not given, abstaining from sexual misconduct. Bhikkhus, this is called right action. And, bhikkhus, what is right livelihood? Here, bhikkhus, a noble disciple, having abandoned wrong livelihood, earns his living by right livelihood. Bhikkhus, this is called right livelihood.

title: And, Puṇṇa, what is neither-dark-nor-bright action with a neither-dark-nor-bright result, action leading to the cessation of action? Here, Puṇṇa, the volition (cetanā) that abandons dark…
content: And, Puṇṇa, what is neither-dark-nor-bright action with a neither-dark-nor-bright result, action leading to the cessation of action? Here, Puṇṇa, the volition (cetanā) that abandons dark action with its dark result, the volition that abandons bright action with its bright result, the volition that abandons dark-and-bright action with its dark-and-bright result — this, Puṇṇa, is called neither-dark-nor-bright action with a neither-dark-nor-bright result, action leading to the cessation of action. Puṇṇa, these four kinds of action have been directly known, realized, and proclaimed by me.

title: Bhikkhus, the eye is impermanent, changing, altering. The ear is impermanent, changing, altering. The nose is impermanent, changing, altering. The tongue is impermanent, changing, altering.…
content: Bhikkhus, the eye is impermanent, changing, altering. The ear is impermanent, changing, altering. The nose is impermanent, changing, altering. The tongue is impermanent, changing, altering. The body is impermanent, changing, altering. The mind is impermanent, changing, altering. Bhikkhus, one who has faith and conviction in these things is called a faith-follower, who has entered the fixed course of rightness, entered the plane of true persons, transcended the plane of ordinary persons. He cannot do any action that would cause him to be reborn in hell, the animal realm, or the ghost realm; he cannot pass away without realizing the fruit of stream-entry. One who, with wisdom, has a degree of acceptance of these things is called a Dhamma-follower, who has entered the fixed course of rightness, entered the plane of true persons, transcended the plane of ordinary persons. He cannot do any action that would cause him to be reborn in hell, the animal realm, or the ghost realm; he cannot pass away without realizing the fruit of stream-entry. One who knows and sees these things as they are is called a stream-enterer, no longer subject to falling, bound for enlightenment.

title: 'Friend, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and they do not experience one another's domain and range. That is, the eye, ear, nose,…
content: 'Friend, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and they do not experience one another's domain and range. That is, the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body. Friend, since these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and do not experience one another's domain and range, what is their support, and what experiences their domain and range?' — 'Friend, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and they do not experience one another's domain and range, namely, the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body. Friend, among these five faculties, the mind is their support, and the mind experiences their domain and range.'
